route.name: Basin and Batholith
route.region: Mammoth Lakes / June Lake / Long Valley
route.description: Public GPX Eastern Sierra volcanic overnighter/long-weekend route with dirt roads, sandy sections, pavement, and high-desert exposure.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Eastern Sierra volcanic overnighter/long-weekend route with dirt roads, sandy sections, pavement, and high-desert exposure.
route.bestSeason: Late spring to early fall; July needs heat and afternoon storm checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Eastern Sierra volcanic overnighter/long-weekend route with dirt roads, sandy sections, pavement, and high-desert exposure.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Mammoth Creek, Owens River, and lakes are key sources; final gaps must be measured.
resources.food: Mammoth Lakes and June Lake are the major resupply anchors.
resources.camping: Wild and developed camping near Obsidian Dome and Big Springs; verify current restrictions.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Conditional July candidate with early starts.
Build a water plan from GPX waypoints.
Avoid exposed sections in afternoon heat.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject/rework under current Inyo/Mammoth closure risk
blockers: Not ride-ready until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Basin and Batholith cannot be ride-ready until the imported GPX is checked against active Inyo/Mammoth-area closure orders and Stage 1 fire restrictions.
Basin and Batholith remains rejected under current closure risk until the GPX is checked against active Inyo Craters, Hot Creek, Shady Rest/Panorama, and Mammoth-area closures.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend ride readiness.
Overlay the GPX against Inyo closure order maps for ESCCRP, Inyo Craters, Mammoth Mill Site, and Mammoth Scenic Loop.
Select July 10-13 camps and verify they are legal under Stage 1 fire restrictions.
Measure GPX water gaps and confirm current water source reliability.
Overlay GPX against current Inyo closure maps.
Create a bypass GPX if any closure overlaps the route.
Confirm exact camp/water plan only after corridor legality is solved.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

days.name: Basin and Batholith segment 1
days.camp: Wild and developed camping near Obsidian Dome and Big Springs; verify current restrictions.

logistics: Final ride-ready status requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Inyo Stage 1 Fire Restrictions checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/inyo/alerts/stage-1-fire-restrictions-effect
Subagent found public GPX and terrain-map artifacts pass, but closure-overlay artifact is still missing.

readinessEvidence.finding: Official alerts list Stage 1 Fire Restrictions, ESCCRP Panorama and Shady Rest closures, Juniper Bike Trail Area Closure, Inyo Craters Closure, Mammoth Mill Site closure, and Mammoth Scenic Loop closure among active alerts.

Stage 1 Fire Restrictions are effective June 22 through December 31, 2026 and restrict fires/stove fires except in listed developed recreation sites; LPG stoves with shutoff valves are allowed with a California Campfire Permit.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Inyo National Forest Stage 1 Fire Restrictions alert

readinessEvidence.finding: Mammoth-area forest health/fuel-reduction closures may affect roads, trails, and recreation areas, so the imported GPX needs corridor-level closure checking.
Subagent reported the route explicitly uses/visits areas implicated by current Inyo/Mammoth-area closures, so the route needs a closure-map overlay before use.
Inyo active alerts include Stage 1 Fire Restrictions plus multiple Mammoth/Hot Creek/Inyo Craters/Shady Rest/Panorama closure orders that must be mapped against the GPX.
Hot Creek has an active closure order page; route source explicitly routes through/near Hot Creek, so corridor legality must be mapped before promotion.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Inyo National Forest Hot Creek Closure Order
